directive: Remove the pg_dump snapshot-consistency test — it reflects Option B, which was not chosen in phase 4. Replace it with a test that validates Option A semantics: simulate an interrupted mid-run by stopping the script after N batches and assert that (a) already-committed batches are durably present in the fixture DB, and (b) re-running the script from scratch is idempotent for those rows via dedup_key (no duplicates in the PD payload output). Also fix docker-compose teardown: add an afterAll (or equivalent) that unconditionally stops and removes the fixture containers even when tests fail, to prevent orphaned containers on CI.
rationale: Two Reviewer rejections. Round 1 (CI budget exceeded) was correctly fixed — 10k rows, 4.1 min, within budget. Round 2 identifies two real defects. First: the snapshot-consistency test is premised on phase 4 choosing Option B (pg_dump snapshot), but the p4 arbitration directed Option A — drop the wrapping transaction, commit per batch, accept snapshot-inconsistency risk, do not introduce a pg_dump step. That test is validating behavior that does not exist in the implementation and will false-pass. Second: docker-compose fixture not torn down on test failure leaves orphan containers, a standard CI hygiene issue. Both are specific and fixable.
